name: Olmeda del Rey — circular desde Almodóvar del Pinar
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Almodóvar Del Pinar navigate a landscape characterized by rolling hills and rural plains, with routes often passing through agricultural areas and near significant water bodies. The region features varied terrain, including moderate ascents and descents, making it suitable for cyclists seeking a mix of challenges. Routes frequently connect small villages and offer views of the local countryside. Best touring cycling routes around Almodóvar Del Pinar The most popular touring cycling route is Hoces del Cabriel and Contreras Reservoir Loop from Motilla del Palancar, a 49.9 miles (80.3 km) trail that takes 5 hours 0 minutes to complete, offering views of the Cabriel gorges and Contreras Reservoir. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Olmeda del Rey — circular desde Almodóvar del Pinar, a moderate 27.1 miles (43.6 km) path. This route traverses rural landscapes and offers a circular journey from Almodóvar del Pinar. Local touring cyclists also love the Paracuellos y Almodóvar del Pinar — circular desde Motilla del Palancar, a 36.8 miles (59.3 km) trail leading through rural areas and small villages, often completed in about 3 hours 35 minutes. Touring cycling around Almodóvar Del Pinar is defined by its rural plains, rolling hills, and proximity to reservoirs. The network offers options for moderate to difficult routes, accommodating different ability levels. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 7 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Almodóvar Del Pinar's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Almodóvar Del Pinar?
A: There are 8 touring cycling routes documented on komoot for the Almodóvar Del Pinar area, offering a variety of experiences for cyclists.

Q: Are there any easy touring bike trails suitable for beginners in Almodóvar Del Pinar?
A: The routes around Almodóvar Del Pinar are generally categorized as moderate to difficult, with no easy routes specifically listed. Cyclists should be prepared for varying terrain, including rolling hills and rural plains.

Q: What is the typical length and duration of touring bike routes in this region?
A: Route lengths vary, with options like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1093980634/olmeda-del-rey-circular-desde-almodovar-del-pinar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Olmeda del Rey — circular desde Almodóvar del Pinar&lt;/a&gt; covering 27.1 miles (43.6 km) in about 2 hours 40 minutes, up to longer routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1086060804/hoces-del-cabriel-and-contreras-reservoir-loop-from-motilla-del-palancar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hoces del Cabriel and Contreras Reservoir Loop from Motilla del Palancar&lt;/a&gt; which is 49.9 miles (80.3 km) and takes around 5 hours.

Q: Are there any circular touring bike routes around Almodóvar Del Pinar?
A: Yes, several routes are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1093980634/olmeda-del-rey-circular-desde-almodovar-del-pinar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Olmeda del Rey — circular desde Almodóvar del Pinar&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1365716918/paracuellos-y-almodovar-del-pinar-circular-desde-motilla-del-palancar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Paracuellos y Almodóvar del Pinar — circular desde Motilla del Palancar&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of challenges can I expect on touring bike routes in Almodóvar Del Pinar?
A: The region features varied terrain with moderate ascents and descents, making it suitable for cyclists seeking a mix of challenges. Routes are categorized as moderate to difficult, often traversing rural landscapes and rolling hills.

Q: What interesting places can I see along the touring cycling routes?
A: Many routes pass through or near charming settlements and historical sites. You might encounter places like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5400705" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almodóvar del Pinar&lt;/a&gt; itself, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6290699" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Motilla del Palancar&lt;/a&gt;, and the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5413585" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Paracuellos Castle&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1086060804/hoces-del-cabriel-and-contreras-reservoir-loop-from-motilla-del-palancar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hoces del Cabriel and Contreras Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt; offers views of the Cabriel gorges and Contreras Reservoir.

Q: Are there any specific landmarks or points of interest to look out for?
A: Beyond the settlements, keep an eye out for the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5413585" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Paracuellos Castle&lt;/a&gt;, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6266927" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente de los Tres Ojos&lt;/a&gt; (Bridge of Three Eyes), and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5413601" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sanctuary of the Virgin of the Loma&lt;/a&gt;, which are notable attractions in the vicinity.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Almodóvar Del Pinar?
A: The region's climate generally makes spring and autumn ideal for cycling, offering milder temperatures compared to the heat of summer. These seasons provide comfortable conditions for exploring the rural plains and rolling hills.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Almodóvar Del Pinar?
A: Cyclists appreciate the varied terrain, which includes both rural plains and rolling hills, and the proximity to significant water bodies like the Contreras Reservoir. The routes often connect small villages, offering a glimpse into the local countryside.

Q: Are there any touring routes that pass through the town of Almodóvar Del Pinar itself?
A: Yes, routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1093980634/olmeda-del-rey-circular-desde-almodovar-del-pinar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Olmeda del Rey — circular desde Almodóvar del Pinar&lt;/a&gt; start and end directly in Almodóvar del Pinar, allowing you to explore the town before or after your ride.

Q: Can I find routes that start from nearby towns like Motilla del Palancar?
A: Absolutely. Several routes originate from Motilla del Palancar, such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1086060804/hoces-del-cabriel-and-contreras-reservoir-loop-from-motilla-del-palancar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hoces del Cabriel and Contreras Reservoir Loop&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1365716918/paracuellos-y-almodovar-del-pinar-circular-desde-motilla-del-palancar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Paracuellos y Almodóvar del Pinar — circular desde Motilla del Palancar&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1086241247/alarcon-circular-from-motilla-de-palancar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alarcón — circular from Motilla de Palancar&lt;/a&gt;.
tips: 18 km section of road that connects the towns of Olmeda del Rey and Almodóvar del Pinar. Asphalt in good condition, little traffic.
Very nice section of the CM-220 road towards Almodóvar del Pinar. Asphalt in perfect condition, good shoulder on the road.
